intro: Dimetia capitellata, also known as Dimetia, is a plant species within the family Lamiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Asia. Traditionally, Dimetia capitellata has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions due to its reported antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Dimetia capitellata include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Dimetia capitellata is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other infections.
